__TR_saas-revenue-quality-score_16__: A composite buyer-side metric that scores SaaS subscription revenue on five dimensions: retention stability, concentration risk, expansion efficiency, payment hygiene, and contract durability.
__TR_saas-revenue-quality-score_17__: MRR is a snapshot. Revenue Quality is the X-ray. Two SaaS companies with $100K MRR can have wildly different risk profiles — one is a compounding asset, the other is a ticking time bomb. The Revenue Quality Score (RQS) tells you which one you're buying.
__TR_saas-revenue-quality-score_18__: Churn Lens computes an RQS for every CSV upload. The score ranges from 0 (failing) to 100 (institutional-grade recurring revenue). Here's how it works.

__TR_saas-revenue-quality-score_30__: Weight: 30% of RQS. Based on NRR, logo churn volatility, and cohort decay slope.
__TR_saas-revenue-quality-score_33__: Weight: 25% of RQS. Based on Revenue HHI, top-5 share, plan tier spread.
__TR_saas-revenue-quality-score_36__: Weight: 20% of RQS. Contraction-adjusted expansion rate, upgrade velocity.
__TR_saas-revenue-quality-score_39__: Weight: 15% of RQS. Involuntary churn rate, failed payment frequency, dunning success rate.

__TR_saas-revenue-quality-score_42__: Weight: 10% of RQS. Annual vs. monthly billing mix, contract length, auto-renewal rate.

__TR_saas-revenue-quality-score_51__: Same MRR, wildly different assets. Company A will grow. If you're paying a multiple on MRR, you're overpaying for Company B unless you've adjusted for the quality gap.
